mnemonics: LINES: Layer Individual Notes for Every Story. (Reminder that line plots are built by layering individual aesthetics like x, y, and group to tell a data story over time.)
keyPoints: Line plots are primarily used to visualize trends and changes in data over a continuous variable, most often time.
The basic function for creating a line plot in ggplot2 is `geom_line()`, which connects observations in order of the x variable.
The `group` aesthetic is critical when plotting multiple lines on the same chart to ensure data points are connected correctly for each category.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use `group` when plotting multiple lines, which results in all data points being connected into a single, chaotic line.
Plotting discrete categorical variables on a continuous scale, which can create misleading or nonsensical line segments between categories.
